Hey guys, JB the wolf is in the house for your weekly intensive. How are you? So I appreciate you bearing with me But I got a really great topic to discuss tonight. I think will really Um Be useful for every single person Who is listening right now doesn't matter what you do for a living Doesn't matter whether you're the top of your game at the bottom whether you work for someone Uh, whether you're a manager or whether you own a massive company. I think that When you listen to this, you'll really understand sort of why you all wear you are right now in your life And also why you're locked into certain patterns of thinking that stop you from really I wouldn't just say being all that you can be being the best you could be but also Just enjoying the journey to getting where you're going And I think there's one thing in my life that I really struggled with Is that is is enjoying the journey and probably if at any point I've I've had it Closest to that point is probably in the last few years where I really I've been enjoying things But still I you know, I tend to stress out and worry myself You know, I wouldn't say sick, but I do a lot of worrying and I I use I call it like my strategy like, you know, it's a fear-based strategy of where I Imagine the worst happening and then sort of like use that as a motivator to make sure it doesn't happen I think a lot of people do that, you know, they have this sort of it's like a fear-based motivation strategy So it's fear doesn't paralyze me when I'm fearful of something when I when I you know, my worst nightmare And I'm not talking my personal life now through my business because that's very different We'll get to that afterwards, right? But in business I worry about, you know, something's not going to work out or I'll lose money or Something's good. A division's going to close like if I worry about that what it does is it focuses me And it sends my mind into overdrive I mean spinning loopy after loop until I come up with some solutions and it allows me to push through any Tardness fatigue I might have it allows me to Um Go through any negativity any barriers to obstacles that that fear Is such a powerful, you know, sort of propulsion Mechanism I move away from it, right as opposed to the other side of the equation, which is pleasure, right? And that means that, you know, we we tend to move towards and we'll pull towards things that we Believe are going to bring us pleasure in life, right? We move towards pleasure get pushed away from pain, right? so What i'm driving at here, right is um Is belief systems? and how the beliefs that you have have a very big part of Of indicating or dictating where you are right now in your life Like for instance one of the things that perplexed me in the early days of me going around the world And teaching the straight line system was that, you know, when I was teaching it at Stratton When I was teaching it at a company that I was running myself and I was there every single day giving meetings, right? In other words, if you just are going out there and rah rah roaring people It doesn't last and it actually backfires It's only when the two are coupled together meaning when you have rah rah rah and you're also actually Teaching them something that changes them meaning makes them better at what they do And again, and then I'm leading up to a very profound thing here And we actually zero in on police because what I'm driving at here Is that you can then look someone in the eye And say to them again and again and again I don't care what happened in the past None of it matters The moment you walked in this door And you say the words I teach you You can be as powerful as the most powerful CEO in America and I would tell my guys to act as if Act that you're a wealthy man rich already. You'll become rich Act that you have confidence and people will have confidence in you and you'll have confidence in yourself Act that you have the answers the answers will come to you. I told them to act as if And that works really well In the presence of upskilling Don't ever forget that the secret sauce To really improving the lives of others is this carefully blended mixture of motivation and skills training You upskill you make them more effective and what that does essentially It gives them The logical reason why not emotional the logical reason why they can look back at their life and they say, okay I I get it that I never was amazing before It's true. It sucks to be alone It hurts to be alone and what happens is that gets locked away in your unconscious mind And it forms patterns around it You start searching for reasons why this seed of the belief you have is true You start acting in accordance with the belief and before you know it The belief forgets more powerful more powerful and almost could become a conviction now in my case It didn't because I was fortunate that I you know I broke out of that belief in probably some very disempowering ways You know by getting very rich and becoming very confident with women when I was younger All right, and then I just you know worked on myself. I worked out and this sort of what do you think guys lift weights? They they get in shape because they want to attract the opposite sex they want to change their own self image of who they were and I Definitely did a massive amount of work and went to therapy as well Right and did all the stuff to change how I viewed myself But don't ever think for a second I want to know that it's always still locked away in there and when you're in your weakest moments When you in you when you're most vulnerable That's when those limiting beliefs will come out and show themselves They they reveal themselves at your weakest moments And then all bets are off And you get something why do I have irrational feelings because they're based on experiences that even though you no longer Apply I'm still running the same old freaking patterns All right now I mentioned that because I didn't want you to like I give you all this good stuff about myself I wanted to do bad stuff too, right enough of that because that's not the point The point is is that I'm talking right now about two things in your life number one I want to ask you about your personal life.
